message.content:
Google’s rich results eligibility is defined by **Google Search Central feature-specific docs**, not by schema.org alone, and Google says you must include all **required** properties for eligibility while **recommended** properties improve display quality.[5] Below is an exhaustive per-feature checklist for the rich-result types you named, using the exact Google Search Central documentation URLs.

| Rich result feature | `schema.org @type` | Required properties for Google rich result eligibility | Key recommended properties | Exact Google Search Central doc URL |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Article** | `Article`, `NewsArticle`, `BlogPosting` | `headline`, `image`, `datePublished`, `dateModified`.[Article docs] | `author`, `publisher`, `mainEntityOfPage`.[Article docs] | `https://developers.google.com/search/docs/appearance/structured-data/article` |
| **Breadcrumb** | `BreadcrumbList` | `itemListElement` with `ListItem` items; each `ListItem` needs `position`, `name`, `item`.[Breadcrumb docs] | No separate recommended properties are called out as eligibility drivers; use valid breadcrumb trail markup.[Breadcrumb docs] | `https://developers.google.com/search/docs/appearance/structured-data/breadcrumb` |
| **Product / product snippet** | `Product` plus `Offer` or `AggregateOffer`; ratings/reviews via `AggregateRating` and `Review` | `name`; and either `offers` (`Offer`/`AggregateOffer`) or `aggregateRating`/`review` for the corresponding enhanced display feature paths, depending on the snippet you want.[Product docs] | `image`, `description`, `sku`, `brand`, `review`, `aggregateRating`, `offers.price`, `offers.priceCurrency`, `offers.availability`, `offers.url`.[Product docs] | `https://developers.google.com/search/docs/appearance/structured-data/product` |
| **FAQ** | `FAQPage` | `mainEntity` with `Question` items; each `Question` needs `name` and `acceptedAnswer`; each `acceptedAnswer` needs `text`.[FAQ docs] | No additional recommended properties are required for eligibility; keep answers directly supported by the page content.[FAQ docs] | `https://developers.google.com/search/docs/appearance/structured-data/faqpage` |
| **Local business** | `LocalBusiness` (or a more specific subtype such as `Restaurant`, `Store`, etc.) | Google’s local-business rich-result guidance centers on a valid `LocalBusiness`-type entity and matching location details; the core structured-data properties used for completeness are `name`, `address`, `telephone`, and `openingHoursSpecification` when applicable.[LocalBusiness docs] | `priceRange`, `image`, `geo`, `sameAs`, `aggregateRating`, `review`, `servesCuisine` for relevant subtypes.[LocalBusiness docs] | `https://developers.google.com/search/docs/appearance/structured-data/local-business` |
| **Event** | `Event` | `name`, `startDate`, `location`.[Event docs] | `endDate`, `eventAttendanceMode`, `eventStatus`, `image`, `description`, `offers`, `performer`, `organizer`.[Event docs] | `https://developers.google.com/search/docs/appearance/structured-data/event` |
| **Recipe** | `Recipe` | `name`, `image`, `recipeIngredient`, `recipeInstructions`.[Recipe docs] | `description`, `author`, `datePublished`, `prepTime`, `cookTime`, `totalTime`, `recipeYield`, `nutrition`, `aggregateRating`, `video`.[Recipe docs] | `https://developers.google.com/search/docs/appearance/structured-data/recipe` |
| **Review snippet** | `Review` or `AggregateRating` embedded on the reviewed item’s schema type | The reviewed item must be marked up and must include either a valid `Review` or `AggregateRating` for the snippet path; the item itself must be one Google supports for review snippets.[Review snippet docs] | `reviewRating`, `author`, `datePublished`, `bestRating`, `worstRating`, plus the reviewed item’s descriptive fields such as `name`, `image`, `brand` where applicable.[Review snippet docs] | `https://developers.google.com/search/docs/appearance/structured-data/review-snippet` |
| **Video** | `VideoObject` | `name`, `thumbnailUrl`, `uploadDate`, `description`.[Video docs] | `contentUrl`, `embedUrl`, `duration`, `expires`, `interactionStatistic`, `publishDate`, `regionsAllowed`, `hasPart`, `potentialAction`.[Video docs] | `https://developers.google.com/search/docs/appearance/structured-data/video` |
| **Organization** | `Organization` | Google’s organization result guidance focuses on a valid organization entity; the core properties used for rich display are `name`, `url`, and `logo`.[Organization docs] | `sameAs`, `contactPoint`, `address`, `foundingDate`, `founder`, `alternateName`, `description`, `telephone`.[Organization docs] | `https://developers.google.com/search/docs/appearance/structured-data/organization` |
| **Sitelinks search box** | `WebSite` plus `SearchAction` | `name`, `url`, and a `potentialAction` of type `SearchAction` with `target` and `query-input`.[Sitelinks search box docs] | `description`, `publisher`, `inLanguage`; for the action, use a working search URL template and a single required query parameter.[Sitelinks search box docs] | `https://developers.google.com/search/docs/appearance/structured-data/sitelinks-searchbox` |

A few important clarifications:

- Google explicitly says to rely on **Google Search Central documentation** as definitive for Search behavior, because schema.org contains many properties that are not required by Google Search.[5]
- For some features, Google’s docs define eligibility at the **feature level** rather than through a single “required properties” list for every schema.org subtype; in those cases, the table above reflects the properties Google uses to determine eligibility and rich-result completeness.[5]
- Some features have **additional subtype-specific constraints** beyond the basic schema properties—for example, review snippets only apply to content types Google supports, and product results have stricter review/offer policies.[Review snippet docs][Product docs]

If you want, I can turn this into a **CSV-style checklist** or a **developer QA matrix** with one row per property and columns for “required / recommended / optional / notes.”
